commit.message:
Handle int overflow in rnn (#28003)

### Description

Fixes two overflow/underflow bugs in the CPU RNN kernel (`rnn.cc`):

- **`SafeInt` for GEMM M-dimension**: `seq_length * batch_size` was
computed as a raw `int64_t` multiply before `narrow<int>()`, meaning an
overflow would be UB before the check could fire. Replaced with
`SafeInt<int64_t>(seq_length) * batch_size` for a checked multiply.

- **`seq_length == 0` guard in `Assign_Y_h`**: For the forward
direction, `last_time_step = seq_length - 1` underflows to `-1` when
`seq_length == 0`, producing a negative `y_offset` and out-of-bounds
read. Added an early-exit that zero-fills Y_h for the direction and
returns. Also handles `sequence_lens[batch] == 0` (same underflow path),
zeroing the affected batch slot and skipping via `continue`.

### Motivation and Context

Silent UB from integer overflow/underflow in shape-derived index
arithmetic can corrupt memory or produce incorrect results without any
diagnostic signal. These cases are legal per the ONNX spec (empty
sequences, per-batch zero-length sequences) and must be handled
explicitly.

---------

commit.message:
Consolidate file path resolution for WebView controls (#34620)

<!-- Please let the below note in for people that find this PR -->
> [!NOTE]
> Are you waiting for the changes in this PR to be merged?
> It would be very helpful if you could [test the resulting
artifacts](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki/Testing-PR-Builds) from
this PR and let us know in a comment if this change resolves your issue.
Thank you!

## Description

Refactored scattered file-path resolution logic in HybridWebView and
BlazorWebView into shared utilities (`FileSystemUtils.Combine`,
`WebUtils.ResolveRelativePath`) to fix edge cases where `Path.Combine`
drops the root directory when a relative path starts with a separator.

### Changes

- **`FileSystemUtils.shared.cs`** — Added `IsValidRelativePath()` and
`Combine()` methods that validate relative paths and ensure the resolved
path stays within the expected root directory
- **`WebUtils.shared.cs`** — Added `ResolveRelativePath()` to resolve
request URIs against an app origin into validated relative paths
- **HybridWebView handlers** (iOS, Windows, Android) — Use the new
shared utilities for file path resolution
- **BlazorWebView file providers** (iOS, Android, Windows, Tizen) — Use
`FileSystemUtils.Combine()` for path resolution
- **`Email.windows.cs`** — Consolidated duplicate `NormalizePath` into
`FileSystemUtils.NormalizePath`
- **Device tests** — Added path resolution tests for both HybridWebView
and BlazorWebView covering relative paths, rooted paths, dot-dot
segments, and encoded separators

Fix sprite batch lighting bounds accumulation

Box3_Union was accumulating in->bounds (the first sprite) instead of
batch->bounds (the current sprite), so R_ActiveLights only considered
the first sprite's position for the entire batch. When that sprite was
far from an explosion, the lighting query found no lights, driving
diffuse to zero and making lit sprites invisible under additive
blending. Since particle order varies per frame, this caused flickering.

commit.message:
fix: prevent 304 bypass of body rewriting via conditional request caching

Two related bugs fixed:

1. Outbound conditional request headers not stripped
   The proxy was forwarding If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Match,
   If-Unmodified-Since, and If-Range to the upstream unchanged.  When the
   browser had a cached copy from a previous proxy run and resent these headers,
   the upstream could return 304 Not Modified.  ModifyResponse's noBody path
   skips all body rewriting on 304 responses, so the browser would then display
   its stale cached copy — which may contain unrewritten upstream hostnames.
   Fix: strip all five conditional headers in the Director before forwarding.

2. ETag + Last-Modified response headers not stripped
   By caching these validators the browser would eventually send conditional
   requests (see bug 1).  Strip both in headersStrip so the browser never has
   a validator to send for proxied resources.

Add TestConditionalRequestHeadersStripped and TestETagLastModifiedStripped.

commit.message:
Fix scheduled task leaving stale segments after analysis (#734)

Remove pushToJellyfin parameter from BaseItemAnalyzerTask.AnalyzeItemsAsync and
the UpdateMediaSegments config gate from the analysis push path.

Previously, DetectSegmentsTask passed pushToJellyfin: false and relied on
Jellyfin's TaskExtractMediaSegments to pull updated segments. This approach
could silently fail if the task wasn't found or wasn't idle, leaving users with
stale segments after analysis.

Now all callers (scheduled task, automatic task, ScanSeason) push newly-analyzed
segments directly via UpdateMediaSegmentsAsync immediately after analysis,
consistent with how the dashboard's ScanSeason option works. The Jellyfin
extraction task trigger is kept as an additional sync for already-analyzed items.

commit.message:
fix: skip body buffering for text/event-stream SSE responses

Server-Sent Events are long-lived streaming responses that must not pass
through io.ReadAll — doing so would block the client from receiving any
events until the stream closes (potentially never for persistent SSE).

Add an early-return in ModifyResponse when Content-Type is text/event-stream.
Header rewriting (host masking, CSP, etc.) still runs before the early exit,
so headers are correctly rewritten even for SSE responses.

FlushInterval=-1 on the ReverseProxy already handles progressive flushing;
this change ensures we don't inadvertently buffer the body first.

Add TestSSEPassthrough: spawns an upstream that writes one event then blocks
on a pipe; asserts the event is readable within 3 seconds (would time out if
the proxy was buffering the full stream).

commit.message:
fix(generator): align = operators in WHERE clause conditions (#49)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Aligns `=` operators in `WHERE` clause conditions so equality
comparisons are easy to scan vertically.

## Root Cause

`where_sql` delegated to `_inline_clause_sql`, which only handles the
leading-`AND` layout. It rendered each condition with natural spacing:

```sql
WHERE p.time_frame = _time_frame
  AND e.participant_key = _participant_key
  AND e.enabled
  AND (
    p.program_supplier_key = _program_supplier_key
    OR _program_supplier_key IS NULL
  )
```

## Fix

`where_sql` now works at the AST level when the top-level is a pure
`AND` chain:

1. Flattens the `AND` chain with `_flatten_and`.
2. Collects all `EQ` nodes anywhere in the `WHERE` tree and computes
`max LHS width + 2` as the alignment target.
3. Pads top-level equality LHS to the target column.
4. Renders top-level `Paren` conditions compactly (single line) via
`_compact_sql`.

```sql
WHERE p.time_frame            = _time_frame
  AND e.participant_key       = _participant_key
  AND e.enabled
  AND (p.program_supplier_key = _program_supplier_key OR _program_supplier_key IS NULL)
```

Falls back to `_inline_clause_sql` when the top-level condition is not
`AND` (e.g. a bare `OR`), preserving existing connector alignment
behaviour.

## Changes

- `src/jarify/generator.py` — new `where_sql`, `_flatten_and`,
`_compact_sql`
- `tests/fixtures/select/where_eq_alignment.{input,expected}.sql` — new
fixture pair
- `tests/fixtures/duckdb/create_macro.expected.sql` — regenerated to
reflect aligned three-EQ WHERE
- `docs/sql-style-guide.md` — new "WHERE — `=` operator column
alignment" section

Resolves #48

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add offline docs.rs-style workspace docs check to Rust CI and fix surfaced rustdoc links (#1295)

Rust CI only validated docs for a single crate, which allowed docs.rs
regressions elsewhere in the workspace. This PR adds a workspace-wide
docs.rs simulation in offline mode so doc breakage is caught before
merge.

- **CI coverage: docs.rs simulation for the full Rust workspace**
- Added `rust::ci-docs-rs` to run a docs.rs-like build across all
workspace crates.
- Uses `DOCS_RS=1`, `--cfg docsrs`, nightly rustdoc, and offline doc
generation.

- **CI integration**
- Wired `ci-docs-rs` into `rust::ci-lint` so the check runs on standard
Rust CI paths.

- **Rustdoc correctness fixes uncovered by workspace-wide docs**
- Fixed broken/private intra-doc links across `mlt-core`, `mlt`,
`mlt-synthetics`, and `mlt-wasm`.
- Replaced invalid links to private or out-of-scope symbols with valid
public links or plain text where appropriate.

```just
ci-docs-rs:
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    set -euo pipefail
    rustup toolchain install nightly --profile minimal
```

fix: strip Report-To, NEL, COOP, COEP, CORP response headers

These headers were forwarded to the browser unchanged, causing two problems:

1. Report-To + NEL: instruct the browser to send network error/timing reports
   directly to upstream endpoints, leaking real hostnames and client traffic
   details even when the proxy successfully masks them from page content.

2. Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy (same-origin) + Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy
   (require-corp) enforce strict cross-origin isolation. In a proxy context
   this breaks subresource loading: COEP blocks resources that lack the
   correct CORP opt-in header, and COOP prevents cross-window communication
   across the proxied origin boundary.

3. Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy (same-origin) blocks the proxy from serving
   cross-origin subresource responses to the browser.

Add all six variants to headersStrip. Extend TestProxyCSPHSTSStripped to
assert all new headers are absent from proxy responses.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: run Vite build before linting to generate Wayfinder type files

Wayfinder generates @/routes and @/actions TypeScript files during
vite build. These are gitignored but required for ESLint to correctly
resolve and order imports. Running build first ensures consistent
ESLint behavior between CI and local dev.
